intent: Check whether the candidate can define Express.js accurately and explain the practical problems it solves compared with using Node.js core http directly.
primaryQuestion: What is Express.js, and what problems does it solve compared with using Node.js core http directly?

expectedSignals.label: Defines Express as a minimal, unopinionated Node.js web application framework for building web apps and APIs.

requirements.description: Defines Express as a Node.js web application framework
Mentions that Express is minimal or unopinionated
Connects Express to building web applications or HTTP APIs

expectedSignals.label: Explains that Node's http module gives low-level request and response primitives, while Express adds routing, middleware composition, request/response helpers, static serving, and error handling conventions.

requirements.description: Explains that Node's http module exposes low-level request and response primitives
Mentions Express routing and middleware composition as added structure
Mentions helpers, static serving, or error handling conventions added by Express

expectedSignals.label: Mentions concrete conveniences such as app.get/app.post, route parameters, req.query, res.status().json(), express.Router, express.json, and centralized error middleware.

requirements.description: Mentions app.get, app.post, or similar route methods
Mentions helpers such as route params, req.query, or res.status().json()
Mentions express.Router, express.json, or centralized error middleware

expectedSignals.label: Understands that Express still runs on top of Node's HTTP server model; it does not remove the need to understand HTTP, async I/O, and request lifecycle.

requirements.description: Explains that Express still runs on top of Node's HTTP server model
Mentions that HTTP concepts still matter in Express
Mentions async I/O or request lifecycle understanding is still needed

expectedSignals.label: Connects the value to less boilerplate, clearer endpoint organization, reusable cross-cutting logic, and faster API development.

requirements.description: Connects Express to reducing low-level HTTP boilerplate
Mentions clearer endpoint or route organization
Connects middleware to reusable cross-cutting logic and faster API development

scoringHints.description: Gives a crisp definition, compares Express with low-level http using concrete examples, and explains the architectural role of routing and middleware.
Understands Express as a Node web framework and names several conveniences, but the comparison with raw http is not very deep.
Knows Express is used for APIs but cannot describe what it adds over Node's built-in HTTP primitives.
Answer is vague or factually wrong about Express, Node.js, or HTTP.

followUps.question: If you implemented GET /users/:id with only Node's http module, what work would you need to do manually that Express gives you directly?
followUps.purpose: Force a concrete comparison between Express routing helpers and low-level HTTP handling.

followUps.question: Which parts of an Express request are still just normal HTTP concepts rather than Express-specific magic?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands Express as a layer over HTTP rather than a separate protocol.

en.title: Express over raw Node http
en.displayQuestion: What is Express.js, and what problems does it solve compared with using Node.js core http directly?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can define Express and explain its value over low-level Node HTTP primitives.
guidance: Start with a precise definition: minimal, unopinionated web framework for Node.js.
Compare against raw http in terms of routing, middleware, request parsing, response helpers, and error handling.
Use a concrete endpoint example such as GET /users/:id.
Mention that Express still relies on Node's HTTP and async runtime model.
commonMistakes: Saying Express is a runtime or a replacement for Node.js.
Only saying it makes things easier without naming concrete mechanisms.
Forgetting middleware and routing.
Assuming raw http is unusable for production.

vi.title: Express so với Node http thuần
vi.displayQuestion: Express.js là gì? Nó giải quyết vấn đề gì so với dùng http module thuần của Node.js?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có định nghĩa đúng Express và giải thích được giá trị của nó so với primitive HTTP cấp thấp trong Node.js hay không.
guidance: Bắt đầu bằng định nghĩa chính xác: framework web tối giản, unopinionated cho Node.js.
So sánh với http thuần qua routing, middleware, request parsing, response helper và error handling.
Dùng ví dụ endpoint cụ thể như GET /users/:id.
Nói rõ Express vẫn dựa trên HTTP server và mô hình async runtime của Node.js.
commonMistakes: Nói Express là runtime hoặc thay thế Node.js.
Chỉ nói Express dễ hơn mà không nêu cơ chế cụ thể.
Quên middleware và routing.
Cho rằng http thuần không thể dùng cho production.

ja.title: Express と Node http の違い
ja.displayQuestion: Express.js とは何ですか。Node.js の core http を直接使う場合と比べて、どの問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express を正しく定義し、低レベルな Node HTTP primitive に対する価値を説明できるか確認する。
guidance: Node.js 向けの minimal で unopinionated な web framework として定義する。
routing、middleware、request parsing、response helper、error handling の観点で raw http と比較する。
GET /users/:id のような具体例を使う。
Express も Node の HTTP server と async runtime model の上にあることを明確にする。
commonMistakes: Express を runtime や Node.js の置き換えと説明する。
具体的な仕組みを挙げずに単に簡単だと言う。
middleware と routing を忘れる。
raw http は production で使えないと決めつける。

Describes NestJS as an opinionated application framework

requirements.description: Chooses NestJS for larger teams or complex domains
Mentions strong conventions or dependency injection as reasons for NestJS
Mentions testing consistency or TypeScript-first architecture

Explains the team must define and enforce project structure

scoringHints.description: Separates abstraction levels, names concrete NestJS mechanisms, and makes a project-context decision with explicit trade-offs.

followUps.question: What does it mean that NestJS uses Express as the default HTTP platform?

en.title: Express versus NestJS
en.displayQuestion: How is Express.js different from NestJS, and when would you choose Express instead of NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate framework trade-off reasoning between a minimal framework and an opinionated application architecture.
guidance: Compare abstraction level first, not popularity.
Mention NestJS modules, controllers, providers, decorators, DI, and adapters.
Give scenarios where Express is enough and scenarios where NestJS conventions pay off.
Call out the responsibility Express pushes back to the team.
commonMistakes: Calling NestJS only Express with decorators.
Saying Express cannot scale.
Ignoring team size, domain complexity, and architectural consistency.
Forgetting that NestJS can run on Express.

vi.title: Express so với NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: Express.js khác gì so với NestJS? Khi nào bạn chọn Express thay vì NestJS?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng reasoning trade-off giữa framework tối giản và framework có kiến trúc ứng dụng rõ ràng.
guidance: So sánh abstraction level trước, không chỉ so sánh độ phổ biến.
Nhắc tới module, controller, provider, decorator, DI và adapter của NestJS.
Đưa tình huống Express là đủ và tình huống convention của NestJS đáng dùng.
Nêu rõ trách nhiệm kiến trúc mà Express đẩy lại cho team.
commonMistakes: Gọi NestJS chỉ là Express có decorator.
Nói Express không thể scale.
Bỏ qua quy mô team, độ phức tạp domain và tính nhất quán kiến trúc.
Quên rằng NestJS có thể chạy trên Express.

ja.title: Express と NestJS
ja.displayQuestion: Express.js は NestJS と何が違いますか。どのような場合に NestJS ではなく Express を選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: minimal framework と opinionated な application architecture の trade-off を説明できるか確認する。
guidance: 人気ではなく abstraction level から比較する。
NestJS の modules、controllers、providers、decorators、DI、adapters に触れる。
Express で十分な場面と NestJS の convention が効く場面を挙げる。
Express が team 側に戻す設計責任を明確にする。
commonMistakes: NestJS を decorator 付き Express とだけ呼ぶ。
Express は scale できないと言い切る。
team size、domain complexity、architecture consistency を無視する。
NestJS が Express 上で動けることを忘れる。

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can compare Express and Fastify in terms of performance model, schema-first design, plugin encapsulation, middleware compatibility, and production trade-offs.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that Fastify is designed around low overhead, schema-based validation/serialization, hooks, and encapsulated plugins, while Express is centered on a simple ordered middleware and routing pipeline.

Mentions Fastify hooks or encapsulated plugins

requirements.description: States that Fastify often benchmarks faster than Express
Connects the advantage to JSON serialization or high-throughput APIs
Mentions measuring database, network, auth, logging, or business logic bottlenecks before migrating

requirements.description: Mentions Express's large historical middleware ecosystem
Mentions Express's familiar req/res APIs
Mentions Fastify's explicit schemas or plugin boundaries

Mentions Fastify hooks and lifecycle differences
Explains that moving between them may require adapting code rather than copy-pasting middleware

expectedSignals.label: Makes a balanced choice: Fastify for performance-sensitive new APIs with schema discipline; Express for ecosystem compatibility, simplicity, existing code, or middleware-heavy apps.

requirements.description: Chooses Fastify for performance-sensitive APIs when measurement supports it
Mentions schema discipline as a good fit for Fastify
Chooses Express for ecosystem compatibility, simplicity, existing code, or middleware-heavy apps

scoringHints.description: Compares pipeline model, schema/serialization, plugin encapsulation, ecosystem compatibility, and benchmark limitations with a production-minded recommendation.

followUps.question: How do JSON schema validation and response serialization affect Fastify's approach?
followUps.purpose: Probe the candidate's understanding beyond benchmark claims.

en.title: Express versus Fastify

guidance: So sánh middleware flow của Express với hook, schema và plugin của Fastify.

Bàn về compatibility middleware và độ trưởng thành hệ sinh thái.

Bỏ qua validation và serialization dựa trên schema.
Cho rằng Express middleware dùng trực tiếp được trong Fastify.
Không nhắc tới đo đạc trong production.
